chunk_summary: Preterm and VLBW infants require structured micronutrient strategies, focusing on iron supplementation and monitoring for metabolic bone disease to prevent deficiencies and complications during rapid growth phases.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of protocolized micronutrient planning for preterm and VLBW infants.
Learn iron supplementation guidelines, including dosage and safety considerations for preterm infants.
Recognize the need for monitoring and managing metabolic bone disease in high-risk infants.
Develop follow-up plans for micronutrient reassessment and documentation.
key_facts: Preterm and VLBW infants require ~2 mg/kg/day of iron during the first year.
Human milk–fed preterm infants often need supplemental iron starting at 2–4 weeks of age.
Infants receiving multiple packed cell transfusions may not require routine iron supplementation due to risk of iron overload.
High-risk infants (birth weight <1500 g, very preterm, prolonged TPN, diuretics/steroids) should undergo serial bone profile monitoring (calcium, phosphate, alkaline phosphatase).
Management of metabolic bone disease depends on distinguishing calcipenic vs phosphopenic patterns.
At discharge, document iron source, vitamin D plan, and set reassessment dates for Hb, ferritin, and bone biochemistry.
reference_quote: Human milk–fed preterm infants typically need supplemental iron starting early (often around 2–4 weeks of age).
document_key_topics: Monitoring and management of metabolic bone disease
Micronutrient follow-up strategies for VLBW infants
